verdict_reason: The four media — śastra, pāvaka, āpaḥ, māruta — are each named individually with a distinct engineering analog. Shankara's partlessness reading (each medium acts via decomposition; the Self has no parts to decompose) is honoured by the framing 'the protocol has no surface for the medium to engage.' Ramanuja's subtlety/pervasion reading (the Self pervades the elements rather than being pervadable by them) is honoured by the framing that a well-specified contract 'floats above the swamp the implementations sink into' — the contract pervades implementations rather than being pervadable by them. The two readings are presented as complementary in the disagreements_among_translators field. No conflation; both traditions are preserved in their distinctive structural-property and topological-pervasion readings. PASS.
